EVENT_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense reported street flooding along highway 2.
Civil Defense reported minor street flooding in Anasco.  Road 402 in Las Marias sector was reported flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense in Mayaguez reported street flooding in the Soledad sector. A house was reported flooded.
Strong winds detached a house zinc roof at the Voladores sector in Moca.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense reported that highway 31 was reported impassable near Naguabo. A house in the Maizales sector of Naguabo was flooded when a nearby creek went out of its banks. Rivers Rio Blanco and Rio Santiago were reported swollen.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense reported downed trees in Anasco and Las Marias. Heavy rain was also reported.
Marble size hail was reported by a resident of Las Marias.
Two funnel clouds were reported near Culebra.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Marble size hail was reported by a residents of Barrio Cacao in Orocovis.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense reported gusty damaging winds, heavy rain, and a funnel cloud in Moca. Downed power lines and trees in the Moca-Aceituna area.  Some of the downed trees damaged some cars.

Heavy rains produced by the passage of a tropical wave, former tropical depression number five, caused flooding in Guaynabo, Bayamon, Toa Baja, Toa Alta, Dorado and Rio Piedras. Rainfall of two to three inches was recorded in parts of the metro area Sunday. Carraizo's water reservoir increased 19 centimeters in 72 hours.

A  tropical wave moved across Puerto Rico and produced heavy rains which caused roads to flood and landslides mainly along the east and northeast sections. The most affected area extended from Rio Grande to Guayama. Many roads were reported impassable like road 906 in Yabucoa, road 3 in front of Palmas del Mar in Humacao, road 198 which leads from Humacao to Las Piedras, road 906, 31, and 191 in Naguabo.

Civil Defense reported heavy rains in the municipalities of Mayaguez, Maricao, San German and Yauco. Doppler radar rainfall accumulation estimates indicated over two inches in Maricao, and about one inch in San German.
A  resident of Barrio Naranjales in Mayaguez reported hail. Strong thunderstorms and heavy rains extended from Jayuya to Mayaguez.
Civil Defense reported that highway 2 in Manati was flooded. Intersection between highway 2 and road 604 was impasable.
The zinc roof of a house at Barrio Atalaya was detached by strong winds.
Thunderstorm winds downed a power pole.
A few thunderstorms produced hay rain over the municialities of Rio Grande westward to Loiza and Carolina.
A pilot reported a waterspout 7 miles southwest of Culebra.
Thunderstorms with very heavy rain produced urban flooding in Puerto Nuevo. Streets 52 and 54 in Reparto Metropolitano were reported flooded as well as parts of the De Diego Avenue.

Thunderstorms that extended from west interior Puerto Rico and from Rincon to Barceloneta produced heavy rains over the municipalities of Ciales, Jayuya, and Orocovis. Around 5.50 inches of rain were reported for Ciales by the rain spotters.
Civil Defense reported very heavy rains near Cayey.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: River Cibuco was reported out of its banks along roads 620, 647, 675 and 676.
Civil Defense reported that a small stream went out of its banks flooding roads 2 and 113 in Isabela.

One person died Tuesday night and another lost his leg when a rock slid off a mountain and crashed into the pickup these persons were riving along highway 156 heading from Naranjito to Comerio in Puerto Rico. Although the area where this incident occurred was not affected by significant flooding, the terrain was saturated due to the rains. This area is along the interior mountaineous section, an area where landslides, mudslides or rockslides are very common during or after heavy rains.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains in Anasco.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains in Aguadilla, Moca and San Sebastian.
Civil Defense reported flooding continued in Aguadilla.
Lanslides were reported in San Sebastian.
Civil Defense reported a small stream out of its banks.
Civil Defense reported very heavy surf along road  #187 from Pinones to Rio Grande. The road was closed off to traffic.
Civil Defense reported that the heavy surf at the river Rio Grande de Arecibo mouth was blocking the normal flow of water to the sea, and the river was rising.
A reporter from Rincon indicated that some moored boats in Aguada were pushed up onto a nearby road at the time of high tide.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense reported that a roof top was detached by the strong winds in Manati.
Civil Defense reported hail in the towns of Arecibo and Hatillo.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The cooperative observer at Barrio Botijas I in Orocovis reported two inches size hail.
A heavy downpour in The Guaynabo's Marina sector caused urban street flooding and forced pedestrians to wade thigh-high to cross one low lying street.

Civil Defense reported that the Cibuco River went out of its banks flooding roads 620 and 647. An automobile and its occupants were swept when the river crested but they were rescued. Roads Trio de 6 in Manati, and 647, 677, and 620 in Vega Alta were impassable.
Civil Defense reported that river Rio Grande de Manati was out of its banks at the Cachete sector. Also Barrio Cortez in Manati was reported flooded, as well as road 642 and the Montebello bridge.
Civil Defense  reported small streams out of their banks along road 951 in Carolina and along road 187, Halcon Viejo sector in Loiza.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains in Orocovis
Civil Defense indicated that flooding was still occuring across Manati, and that most of the rivers were near bankful.
Civil Defense rported heavy rains in the Fajardo Luquillo area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The remnants of a cold front produced heavy rains across the San Juan metropolitan area. Civil Defense reported flooding in Ocean Park and Punta Las Marias sectors in the San juan metropolitan area and over the municipalities of Dorado and Toa Baja.
Civil Defense reported mud slides in Utuado produced by heavy rains, also frequent lightning with strong and gusty winds was reported about 40 minutes earlier.
An observer reported multiple waterspouts just north of Culebra.
Heavy rains produced several small streams out of their banks in the town of Loiza. In addition route 187 was flooded and closed to traffic.
A weather observer reported two waterspouts and a funnel cloud five  miles offshore in the southeast coast of Puerto Rico between Guayama and Patillas.
A waterspout was reported by an observer five miles east of Fajardo.
Civil Defense reported minor flooding in the town of Toa Alta.
Civil Defense reported minor flooding of roads, streets and streams across Fajardo.
A weather observer reported a waterspout between Fajardo and Margarita island.
Civil Defense reported Baldorioty expressway in Santurce was flooded.
A waterspout was reported one mile northwest of Culebra.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: April 1997 was the driest April ever in San Juan...and the second driest month. Many towns went through the entire month without any rain. During this dry spell which started on March 15, 1997 the north coast and north slopes only recorded 14-15 percent of the normal rain. Islanwide only 19 percent of the normal rain was registered during this period. Only 0.08 inches of rain were registered at the Luis Munoz Marin International airport, shattering the previous record of 0.28 inches in April 1984.
Civil Defense reported that a river in San Lorenzo overflowed its banks near road #81 at Barrio Espinoza.
A pilot at the Ramey Airport reported seeing a funnel cloud with a possible tornado near the end of the runway at the airport.
A pilot reported a waterpout 3 miles southwest of the Culebra lighthouse.

Heavy swells caused coastal flooding along the exposed coastal areas of northern Puerto Rico. The most impacted section was in Loiza...Pinones. Road 187 was closed several times at the time of high tide due to sea water covering the road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense reported minor flooding in the town of Comerio.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Police department reported heavy rains in Villalba.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains in Sabana Grande.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains in Ciales and Morovis.
A pilot reported a waterspout 1 nautical mile north of Culebra.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Flight Service Station reported three waterspouts three miles west of Culebra.
Civil Defense reported minor street and streams flooding in the town of Ceiba.
A small craft operators reported two waterspouts near Ramos island three miles east of Fajardo.
Civil Defense reported minor flooding in the town of San Sebastian.
A pilot reported a waterspout near Palomino island east of Fajardo.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A pilot reported a waterspout 2 miles northwest of Culebra.
Civil Defense and Police reported heavy rains.
Civil Defense reported heavy rains in the municipalities of Carolina, Trujillo Alto, Canovanas and Fajardo.

A person was dragged by the undercurrent in the Colorada sector of the Seven Seas beach in Fajardo and was reported missing. Three other tourists who were snorkeling were rescued by authorities after they were carried away by the strong currents.
Civil Defense reported coastal flooding along road #187 from Loiza to Pinones.
Civil Defense reported heavy rain and street flooding in Bayamon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: December was a very dry month. Only 1.02 inches of rain were recorded at San Juan making this the second driest December on record. New record high temperatures for the day in San Juan were established on the 2nd, 15th, and 30th.  Island wide the rainfall average was 1.24 inches. This represent three inches below normal and the lowest rainfall average for the month of December since 1963.  The major weather system responsible for this dry and warm weather was a nearly stationary high pressure system that prevented the arrival of cold fronts to our area.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains were reported in the municipality of Anasco.
Civil Defense  reported flash flooding in the Guayama municipality.

Hurricane Erika was the only named tropical cyclone to be tracked in September. It formed in the central Atlantic on the 3rd, strengthened to a hurricane on the 5th and passed northeast of the U.S. Virgin Islands on the 6th and 7th as it continued to stregthen. Erika's strongest winds missed the U.S.V.I. but the outer rainbands caused gusty winds. A tropical storm watch was issued for the U.S.V.I at 530 am ast on the 5th and was upgraded to a hurricane watch at 11 am ast on the 5th.  The hurricane watch remained in effect until 5 pm ast on the 7th. Sustained winds of 37 mph from the west northwest with gusts to 47 mph were recorded at the St. Thomas airport on the 7th. Up to 3.28 inches of rain were registered at the University of the V.I., elsewhere rainfall varied from 1.41 inches at Windswept Beach to 3.12 inches at Estate Wintberg. These rains produced localized street flooding at Long Bay. Around 1.32 inches were recorded at St. John. Storm effects consisted of power fluctuations.  There were reports at Water Island that one dinghy sank at the dock and a 50 foot boat had to be rescued on Sunday afternoon.
A pilot reported a waterspout five miles south of Culebra.
Thunderstorms produced very heavy rains from Yauco and Maricao westward to Hormigueros.
Showers an thnuderstorms accompanied by heavy rains occurred across the muncipalities of Coamo, Penuelas, northern Ponce, and from Jayuya west Anasco.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Erika ws the only named tropical cyclone to be tracked in September. It formed in the central Atlantic on the 3rd, strengthened to a hurricane on the 5th, and passed just northeast of the U.S.V.I. and P.R. on the 6th and 7th as it continued to strengthen. Erika's strongest winds missed P.R. but the extreme outer rainbands caused gusty winds and some rains across P.R.  A hurricane watch was issued for Puerto Rico, Vieques and Culebra at 11 am ast  on the 5th which remained in effect until 5 pm ast on the 7th. Maximum sustained winds of 23 mph from the west were recorded at the Luis Munoz Marin Airport on the 7th. A peak gust of 42 mph was recorded on the afternoon of the 6th. Rainfall across P.R varied from 0.29 inches at Carolina to 0.77 inches at Caguas. Northeast swells of ten to twelve feet produced some coastal flooding and beach erosion along the north coast. Eight families were evacuated from their homes in the municipality of Dorado's Costa de Oro Urbanization. On Sunday road 187 in Pinones Loiza was closed after sections of the road were flooded or washed by the sea. Wind gusts associated to the outer spiral bands snapped tree branches disrupting electrical service in sectors of San Juan, Guaynabo and Bayamon. Power failures affected ten to twelve thousand residents.

Strong thunderstorms with torrential rains were detected by the doppler radar from Adjuntas west to Lares. The river Rio Arecibo rain gauge in Adjuntas recorded in excess of five inches of rain and the Civil Defense office in Adjuntas recorded 5.82 inches.
Street flooding was reported in Bayamon.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: River Rio Naguabo was reported out of its banks.
Highway 3 was reported impassable from Salinas to Guayama.
River Rio Blanco was reported out of its banks.  Police reported waters rising rapidly at Barrio Mariana, Quebrada Palma sector.
River Rio Fajardo was reported out of its banks in some places.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain and street flooding was reported in Toa Alta.
The Quebrada Ana creek was reported swollen.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense reported small streams out of their banks and flooded houses in the municipalities of Humacao, Guayama, and Naguabo.
Urban flooding was reported at Las Vegas urbanization.
River Rio Fajardo was reported out of its banks near the Union and Hatillo streets.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Erika strongest winds missed the U.S.V.I. but the outer rainbands caused gusty winds. Sustained winds of 25 mph from the west southwest were recorded at the St. Croix airport with gusts to 29 mph. Rainfall varied from 0.83 inches at Christiansted to 0.25 inches at the USDA meteorological station. Storm effects were mostly downed and broken power lines from wind gusts.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quebrada Tres Muertos creek went out of its banks flooding the neighborhoods of Mabu, Los Rosales, San Francisco, Ext. Roig, Paraizo, Villa de Oriente, and the fire station.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Mayaguez Civil Defense reported urban flooding of low lying areas.
A few thunderstorms produced heavy rains across the San Juan metropolitan area. Civil Defense reported street flooding in Hato Rey, Puerto Nuevo, and Rio Piedras.
A pilot reported a funnel cloud one mile north of Palomino Island.

Hurricane Erika was the only name tropical cyclone to be tracked in September. It formed in the central Atlantic on the 3rd, strengthened to a hurricane on the 5th and passed just northeast of the U.S.V.I. on the 6th and 7th as it continued to strengthen. A hurricane watch was issued for Puerto Rico, Vieques and Culebra at 1130 am ast on the 5th which remained effect until 5 pm ast Sunday the 7th.  Erika's strongest winds missed P.R. but the extreme outer rainbands caused gusty winds and some rain. Maximum sustained winds of 23 mph from the west northwest were recorded at the Naval Station in Roosevelt Roads Ceiba on the afternoon of the 6th with a peak gust of 35 mph. Recorded rainfall was 0.52 inches on the 6th.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains produced urban flooding over the San Juan metropolitan area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Civil Defense reported that road 31 in Naguabo was impassable due to the heavy rains. Rio Fajardo river went out of its banks and highway 3 was impassable near the Fajardo Airport.
River Mameyes in Rio Grande went out of its banks and flooded nearby houses, roads and streets.  In Trujillo Alto the Guayabal sector  was flooded.

Strong thunderstorms produced heavy rains in the municipality of Utuado. Civil Defense reported around four inches of rainfall which caused ten lanslides and two creeks to overflow. One house was reported flooded, and fifteen families were isolated at Barrio Paso Palmas due to a lanslide.
A small stream flooded road 402.

A strong thunderstorm...possibly severe and located about 10 miles northeast of Mayaguez produced  heavy rains in the municipality.  Street flooding was reported in Alturas de Mayaguez.  A mudslide was also reported at Barrio Miradero.

Showers and thunderstorms associated to a  tropical wave began to pound the southeast section of Puerto Rio during the early morning. By late morning the rains spreaded to the northeast municipalities of Luquilo and Rio Grande. Civil Defense in Naguabo reported river Rio Blanco out of its banks and water covering highway 191.  Lightning provoked power outages in several areas across the island. The amount of rainfall over the Carraizo basin caused the water level to increase above the maximum and the floodgates of the lake were opened.
A strong thunderstorm produced heavy rains. Around three inches of rainfall were reported for the three hour period beginning at 12 noon.
Three houses were flooded in Coamo.
Civil Defense reported heavy rain in San Sebastian,  Moca, and Aguada.

Strong thunderstorms produced heavy rains across the municipality of Anasco. Civil Defense reported creeks that overflowed their banks and landslides. Three houses were flooded at Barrio Miraflores. A tree was downed along road 402, km. 7.6.

A band of heavy showers and thunderstorms that extended from Barceloneta to Bayamon produced street flooding in Levittown Toa Baja, Catano, Bayamon, Toa Alta, Dorado and Guaynabo. Street flooding was also reported near Plaza Las Americas in Hato Rey.
Civil Defense reported torrential rains in Barrio Punta Santiago and Anton Ruiz of Humacao.
Civil Defense reported downed trees and power poles in Anasco.
Utuado's Civil Defense reported 5.00 inches of rain during the afternoon. River Rio Vivi rose 4-5 feet. The rains produced several landslides but no flooding problems were reported.
Street flooding was reported in Las Piedras.
Civil Defense reported that road 31 in Naguabo was impassable due to the heavy rains. Rio Fajardo river went out of its banks and highway 3 was impassable near the Fajardo Airport.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A nearly stationary low level trough produced heavy rains across the east sections of Puerto Rico. This  rainfall began the night of  October 13 and continued until the morning of October 14.  Widespread flooding and landslides occurred across most of the east. State Civil Defense reported  one person missing(Mr. Jesus Nieves-Garcia), apparently washed away by a wall of water from a bridge that collapsed, in Barrio Jagual in San Lorenzo. Several communities  were incommunicated and 20 persons were in shelters in Salinas and 17 persons in Patillas.  Rivers out of their banks were reported  in Naguabo, Juncos, and Santa Isabel.  Roads impassable due to flooding or landslides were in Naguabo, Aguas Buenas, Juncos, Yabucoa, Gurabo, Caguas, Patillas, Maunabo, Cayey, Guaynabo,  Cidra and Guayama.  Several houses  were  flooded in Gurabo and Juncos, and various bridges  collapsed in  San Lorenzo, Juncos, and Cayey.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Minor street flooding was reported in Vieques.
River Mameyes in Rio Grande went out of its banks and flooded nearby houses, roads and streets.  In Trujillo Alto the Guayabal sector  was flooded.
Heavy rains produced a landslide in road 10 in Utuado.
A weather service employee reported a funnel cloud a few miles northeast of Luis Munoz Marin  International Airport in San Juan.
Heavy rains produced flooding of streets in Naguabo. Roads 31 and 927 were reported  impassable.
State civil defense reported heavy rains and urban flooding in portions of Santurce and Carolina.
State civil defense reported heavy rains and urban flooding in portions of Santurce and Carolina.
Heavy rains produced flooding over road #3 in Fajardo, and roads #31 and #927 in Naguabo. A few residences were flooded in Luquillo and Rio Grande.
Heavy rains produced flooding over road #3 in Fajardo, and roads #31 and #927 in Naguabo. A few residences were flooded in Luquillo and Rio Grande.

Showers and thunderstorm associated with a tropical wave interacting with an upper level low produced heavy rains over the eastern section of Puerto Rico. Around four inches of rain fell in Humacao and road 831 was closed due to flooding.
Coast Guard reported a waterspout off the coast of Fajardo.

A band of strong thunderstorms that extended from Loiza to Toa Alta caused heavy rains that flooded some streets in Condado, Ocean Park and Barrio Obrero in  Santurce. A house at Enia Street in Villa Palmeras lost its zinc roof and was reported flooded. A tree was partialy downed at Cacique Street in Ocean park.
